role: 3rd Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic of Iran (2026–present)
bio: Mojtaba Khamenei is the second son of [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) and has been a shadowy but powerful figure in the Islamic Republic for decades. He long headed an intelligence bureau within the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps), functioning as a key conduit between his father's office and the IRGC's security apparatus. His wife is Zahra Haddad-Adel, daughter of [Gholam-Ali Haddad-Adel](/people/gholam-ali-haddad-adel), former Speaker of the [Islamic Consultative Assembly](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly). Following Ali Khamenei's death on 28 February 2026, the [Assembly of Experts](/orgs/assembly-of-experts) elected Mojtaba as the third Supreme Leader on 8 March 2026 — the second succession in the republic's history.
editorial_notes: Born 1969. Married Zahra Haddad-Adel, daughter of Gholam-Ali Haddad-Adel. Chosen as Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts on 8 March 2026. Lacked marja' credential, mirroring the 1989 precedent with his father.
